title: Oportun Financial Q4 2023 Earnings Call Highlights
text: Oportun achieved $25 million of GAAP net income in 2023, marking the fifth consecutive profitable quarter. The company reported Q4 diluted EPS of $0.07, with adjusted EPS of $0.27. Full-year adjusted EPS reached $1.36, representing an 89% increase year-over-year. Fourth-quarter annualized net charge-offs stood at 12.3%, described as at the low end of the company's guidance range. The 30+ delinquency rate reached 4.9%. Management guided toward a peak charge-off rate in Q1 2024 of 12.65% ±15 basis points, with expected moderation beginning in Q2. Operating expenses totaled $84 million in Q4, the lowest quarterly spend as a public company. Full-year OpEx decreased 12% to $362 million. The adjusted operating expense ratio achieved a record low of 11.6%, exceeding the company's 12.5% unit economics target for the first time. Oportun reduced high-cost corporate debt by $70 million and raised $1.9 billion in the ABS market at sub-6% yields over nine months. The company increased warehouse capacity to $1.14 billion. 2024 Guidance: Q1 2024 revenue: $225-$230 million. Full-year revenue: $935-$955 million. Full-year adjusted EPS: $1.50-$1.65.
